project.objective: Who were the parents of Christiana Clark, born 29 January 1783 in Pennsylvania, who married William Clark, lived in Coshocton County, Ohio, and died 27 December 1848 in Monroe, Green County, Wisconsin?

questions.question: What do death, burial, and probate records for Christiana Clark (died 27 December 1848, Monroe, Green County, Wisconsin) reveal about her birth parentage or Pennsylvania origins?
questions.rationale: Death-proximate records (death certificate, burial register, probate inventory/will) are the logical starting point because they are closest in time to her death, may directly name parents or birth county, and anchor identity before building on Ohio and Pennsylvania searches. Wisconsin became a state in 1848 and early vital records, county court probate files, and church burial registers exist for Green County. This is the highest-yield gatekeeper step.

questions.question: What does the church baptism record for Christina Clark (baptized 2 March 1783, Rehrersburg, Tulpehocken Township, Berks County, Pennsylvania) name as her parents?
questions.rationale: The tree already records a specific baptism date and place — 2 March 1783, Rehrersburg, Tulpehocken Township, Berks County, PA. German Reformed and Lutheran congregations in Tulpehocken Township kept detailed baptismal registers that name both parents and godparents. FamilySearch has microfilmed and indexed many Berks County church records. This question can be pursued autonomously right now and is the most direct path to naming Christiana's parents. Q_001 (Wisconsin death/burial/probate) is awaiting external-site captures from the user; this question is independent of those results.

exhaustive_declaration.justification: The original Tulpehocken German Reformed church baptismal register image (src_003, log_013) directly names father Adam Schreck and mother Margaretha as parents of Christina Schreck, born 29 January 1783, baptized 2 March 1783, Rehrersburg, Tulpehocken Township, Berks County, Pennsylvania. Two independent index transcriptions (src_001, src_002) corroborate the parentage. The date conflict (c_001: 2 Mar vs. 3 Feb 1783) is resolved by the original image. A dedicated parents' marriage search (pli_009) was executed across four FamilySearch variations (log_014–log_017), all nil — the Tulpehocken marriage registers for 1775–1785 are not in FamilySearch's indexed or fulltext-searchable collections; absence documented as a known limitation, not a failure. Mother's maiden surname is not recoverable from available online sources; this limitation is honestly stated. All plan items completed or skipped with rationale. GPS mentor verdict: looks_solid.

stop_criteria.goal_alignment: Yes — original church register directly names father Adam Schreck and mother Margaretha as parents of Christina Schreck, who is identified as Christiana Clark (GP3R-215) by convergence of birth date (29 Jan 1783), baptism date (2 Mar 1783), and parish (Rehrersburg, Tulpehocken). The question is fully answered.
stop_criteria.repository_breadth: FamilySearch birth/christening collections searched across two strategies (log_011, log_012); original register image read (log_013); marriage searched across 4 FamilySearch variations including both indexed and fulltext-searchable collections (log_014–017). Ancestry fallback (pli_011) appropriately skipped given positive FamilySearch result. HSP physical repository noted as option for mother's maiden surname but not required for q_002's scope.
stop_criteria.original_substitution: Yes — assertions a_001–a_004 are anchored to src_003, the original church register image (source_classification: 'original'). Derivative indexes src_001 and src_002 used only for discovery; original image is the primary citation.
stop_criteria.independent_verification: Two index transcriptions (src_001, src_002) both name Adam Schreck as father; they are dependent derivatives of the same underlying register, not independent informants. However, the original register image (src_003) is the primary source and stands on its own authority as a contemporaneous record. The convergence of birth date, baptism date, and parish across both indexes confirms the identity match with high confidence. A second entirely independent source type (e.g., a will or census naming Adam Schreck's children) would strengthen the proof but is not accessible for 1783 Pennsylvania.
stop_criteria.evidence_class: Yes — src_003 is an original record (digital image of the handwritten church register) with primary information (clergyman recorded the baptism contemporaneously; parents were present and named). Assertions a_003 and a_004 are direct evidence of paternity and maternity.
stop_criteria.conflict_resolution: Yes — c_001 (baptism date 2 Mar vs. 3 Feb 1783) resolved by original register image. Assertion a_005 (3 Feb 1783) rejected as indexer transcription error. No other conflicts identified.
stop_criteria.overturn_risk: Low. The original church register is the authoritative source for this event; no other record type exists that would name different parents for an 1783 Pennsylvania baptism. Marriage search nil is documented across 4 variations — mother's maiden surname remains unknown but does not affect the answer to q_002. The only material gap (Ancestry German Reformed PA collection, pli_011) is a fallback that would not change the parentage finding.

items.rationale: Wisconsin Death Index, 1820-1907 (collection 1940759) is the fastest free indexed search. Although systematic state death registration began in 1852, this index draws on county clerk records and compiled sources and may capture pre-1852 entries for Green County. A hit would confirm Christiana's death date and location and possibly name informants.

Wisconsin Deaths and Burials, 1835-1968 (collection 1708699) is a complementary compiled index that incorporates church burial registers and cemetery transcriptions. It may contain entries not in the death index (collection 1940759), particularly for pre-registration deaths from religious communities in Monroe.

Search Find a Grave and the Wisconsin Gravestone Photo Project (wisconsingravestones.org) for Christiana Clark in Green County. Gravestones from this era sometimes record birthplace, age at death, or parents' names. Cemetery transcriptions are free and may also confirm a burial companion (William Clark) whose death record could then be searched for family information.

Wisconsin Probate Estate Files, 1848-1948 (collection 1874190) holds Green County probate packet images from the year of statehood. County probate courts were organized in 1848. Search for both Christiana Clark (she may have had an estate if she held property) and William Clark (if he survived her, his file would name heirs and could identify Christiana's origins or parents; if he predeceased her, her file would be the target). Probate inventories, petitions, and guardian bonds name relatives and frequently state birthplaces.

Ancestry: Wisconsin Wills and Probate Records, 1800-1987 (collection 9088) — paid fallback for the FamilySearch probate search (pli_004). May have an index or different image set that makes it easier to locate Clark estate packets within the browse-only FamilySearch collection.

MyHeritage Wisconsin Death Index, 1808-1909 — a third compiled death index sourced independently of FamilySearch collections. Useful if pli_001 and pli_002 return nothing; different source transcriptions sometimes catch entries missed elsewhere.

Identity-bridge priority: if Adam Schreck left a will in Berks County naming his daughter Christiana (later Clark), that is the strongest possible independent link between the Tulpehocken Christina Schreck (baptized 1783) and the Wisconsin Christiana Clark (died 1848). Adam Schreck was father of a child born 1783; if he was born ~1750-1760 he could have died 1800-1840. Pennsylvania Wills and Probate Records, 1683-1993 are indexed on FamilySearch. A will naming a daughter Christiana Clark would close the identity gap the mentor flagged.

FAN/sibling approach: searching the same HSP baptism collection (4138679) for other children of Adam Schreck (fatherGivenName Adam, fatherSurname Schreck, Berks County, 1770-1800) will establish the Schreck sibling group. If multiple children of Adam Schreck and Margreth are documented in Berks County, and one of them is Christina/Christiana later identified as Christiana Clark, the family group corroborates the identity claim. Siblings also provide context for resolving whether Christiana/Christina is the same person as the Wisconsin decedent.

Marriage record for William Clark and Christiana/Christina would be a direct identity anchor: a Pennsylvania marriage record naming the bride as Christina Schreck (or daughter of Adam Schreck) marrying William Clark would close the Schreck-to-Clark gap definitively. If they married in Berks County (where the Schrecks lived) or an adjacent county before moving to Ohio, the record would be in FamilySearch collection 1681011 (Pennsylvania Marriages 1709-1940). Searching William Clark + Christiana/Christina, PA, 1800-1820.

Ohio census records (1820, 1830, 1840) for William Clark in Coshocton County establish the household and confirm a woman of approximately Christiana's age (born 1783: age ~37 in 1820, ~47 in 1830, ~57 in 1840) in the household. While pre-1850 censuses do not name individuals other than the head, age-bracket confirmation together with the family's documented residence in Coshocton County supports the identity argument as corroborating circumstantial evidence.

FamilySearch collection 1681005 'Pennsylvania, Births and Christenings, 1709-1950' is fully indexed with 2.5M records and directly covers Berks County church baptisms. The tree already shows Christiana was baptized 2 March 1783 at Rehrersburg, Tulpehocken Township — German Reformed and Lutheran registers from this community routinely name both parents (father's full name, mother's maiden name). A match here would be direct evidence naming both parents.

FamilySearch collection 4138679 'Pennsylvania, Philadelphia, Historical Society of Pennsylvania, Births and Baptisms, 1520-1999' (1.29M records) is a separate index that may include Berks County German Reformed and Lutheran baptisms not captured in collection 1681005. Fallback if pli_007 returns nil.

For a parentage question, planning standards require a dedicated search for the candidate parents' marriage to each other. The parents' marriage record in Berks County's German Reformed or Lutheran registers would supply the mother's maiden name — information census and death records almost never carry. FamilySearch collection 1681011 'Pennsylvania, Marriages, 1709-1940' (661,399 records) covers this jurisdiction. A Christiana born 2 March 1783 would have parents who married roughly 1778-1784.

Volume_search returned digitized Tulpehocken church registers not accessible through the indexed collection search — Berks County German Reformed church records (Rehrersburg congregation) may exist as browse-only image groups. If the index search (pli_007/pli_008) returns nil or the match is uncertain, browsing the original register images for the 1783 baptism entry would provide the authoritative primary source. Use image_search to identify specific DGS numbers and then read the register pages.

Ancestry holds Pennsylvania church and vital records collections that may index Berks County German Reformed baptisms independently. If FamilySearch collections are exhausted without a match, Ancestry's 'Pennsylvania, Births and Christenings, 1709-1950' or 'U.S., German Reformed Church Records, Pennsylvania, 1683-1960' may capture the Rehrersburg register. Researcher has Ancestry subscription on file.

log.notes: 477 results returned but all from modern collection 'United States, Residence Database, 1970-2024' — collection mismatch, not relevant. Wisconsin formal death registration began 1852; Christiana died December 1848. Collection 1940759 (Wisconsin Death Index, 1820-1907) returned 0 results across prior two queries. Pre-registration death records must be sought in burial/church registers (pli_002) and probate (pli_004).
Zero results. Trying variant: givenName Christina, no death year filter.
Zero results with Christina variant and Green County death place. Trying surname-only with spouse William to cast wider net.

4 results returned, all clearly wrong (male subjects, wrong era 1870-1909, wrong county). No entry for Christiana Clark (female, b. 1783 PA, d. 27 Dec 1848 Green Co. WI). Collection 1708699 has sparse pre-1852 coverage — Wisconsin formal death registration did not begin until 1852. Pre-registration deaths in Monroe were likely recorded only in church registers (Monroe M.E. Church records start 1853, postdating her death). Proceeding to probate search (pli_004) as most promising source.

query.note: Multiple searches — collection 1874190 images-only (no index), fulltext_search returned 0 across 6 queries (with/without collection filter, with/without place filter, specific imageGroupNumbers 004659615_014_M9V2-C41 and 004659615_001_M9V2-CWG), probate record_search for William Clark WI 1848-1870 also 0

log.notes: Wisconsin Probate Estate Files, 1848-1948 (collection 1874190) is images-only with no searchable index. Fulltext search returned 0 results across all query combinations including specific imageGroupNumbers from volume_search — AI transcription pipeline may not have processed these volumes yet. Manual image browse required; this is best pursued via Ancestry collection 9088 (pli_005) which may have a separate index. No probate record for William Clark found in any Wisconsin record_search (probate type, 1848-1870).
URL generated for Find a Grave; awaiting user capture. Also includes Wisconsin Gravestone Photo Project as a free alternative.

URL generated for Ancestry Wisconsin Wills and Probate Records (collection 9088); awaiting user capture. Two searches needed: one for Christiana Clark's estate, one for William Clark's estate (as surviving spouse he may have filed after her death, or his own later probate names children and Christiana).

URL generated for MyHeritage Wisconsin Death Index 1808-1909; awaiting user capture. Independent indexing from FamilySearch; may surface pre-registration entries from county records or compiled sources not in FS collections.

Collection mismatch: all 13 results are from collection 2608510 (GenealogyBank newspaper births 1815-2014), not the targeted col. 1681005 Pennsylvania Births and Christenings. The collection filter was specified but did not restrict results — engine returned no Pennsylvania church baptism records for Christina Clark. Attempting broader search without surname to find any Christina baptized in Berks County 1783.

CRITICAL MATCH: Results 19 and 20 are Christina Schreck, born 29 January 1783, baptized 2 March 1783 at Rehrersburg, Tulpehocken Township, Berks, Pennsylvania — Father: Adam Schreck, Mother: Margreth (Schreck). This matches all three known attributes of Christiana Clark exactly: birth date 29 Jan 1783, baptism date 2 Mar 1783, baptism place Rehrersburg Tulpehocken. Her maiden name was Schreck, not Clark — she took Clark surname on marriage to William Clark. Two entries (results 19 from collection 4138679 HSP Births/Baptisms, and result 20 also col 4138679) represent the same baptism record with slight date variant in mother's name. Need ARKs to read full records.

query.collection: Tulpehocken church register, Berks County PA, 1783

log.notes: Original Tulpehocken church register image read and transcribed. Entry 6 on the page: birth 29 January 1783 (29ten Jenner), baptism 2 March 1783 (2ten Mertz), child Christina, father Adam Schreck, mother Margaretha. Sponsors include at least one Schreck-surnamed sponsor and female sponsor Christina. RESOLVES date conflict: original register confirms baptism on 2 March 1783. The '3 February' date in src_002 (6CTR-VMZX) was an indexer misread of the baptism/birth columns. German language, Kurrent script. Confirms parentage: father Adam Schreck, mother Margaretha (Margreth).

Nil. Pennsylvania Marriages collection 1681011 (1709-1940) contains no indexed entry for Adam Schreck marrying in Berks County 1775-1785. Collection may not index pre-Revolutionary German Reformed church records at this level of detail for Tulpehocken Township.

140 total matches but none relevant — all from Philadelphia 19th-20th century records or other wrong jurisdictions/eras. No Adam Schreck + Margaretha marriage in Pennsylvania 1770s-1780s appears in FamilySearch indexed collections.

Nil. Fulltext search for Adam + Schreck + Margreth in Berks County PA 1770-1790 returns nothing. AI-transcription pipeline has not processed Tulpehocken marriage registers for this period, or these records are not in FamilySearch's fulltext index.

Nil. Broadening to Adam + Schreck in Berks County PA 1760-1795 still returns nothing. FamilySearch fulltext index does not appear to have AI-transcribed the Tulpehocken German Reformed church marriage registers for this period. The marriage of Adam Schreck to Margaretha is not recoverable through FamilySearch indexed or fulltext-searchable collections. This is a documented limitation — 'not found online' does not mean the record does not exist. The Tulpehocken church registers may be accessible through the HSP (Historical Society of Pennsylvania) or physical repositories.

Nil. Adam Schreck's probate is not in FamilySearch's indexed Pennsylvania probate collections for 1795-1850. The Berks County German Reformed community may have left estate records at the county courthouse (Reading, PA) that have not been digitized or indexed by FamilySearch. The highest-value identity bridge (a Schreck will naming daughter Christiana Clark) remains unexamined in the physical repository. This is a documented limitation — not found online does not mean the record does not exist.

3 results: (1) Christina Schreck — born 29 Jan 1783, baptized 2 Mar 1783, Rehrersburg [already known, src_001/src_003]; (2) Anna Maria Schreck — born 21 Sep 1777, baptized 23 Dec 1777, Rehrersburg, Tulpehocken Township, Berks County, father Adam Schreck [NEW SIBLING]; (3) Christina Schreck — same child, second index entry [src_002, already known]. Anna Maria Schreck (KZ7H-P63 in FamilySearch tree) is a confirmed sister of Christina Schreck in the same Rehrersburg parish with the same father Adam Schreck. This corroborates the Schreck family group at Rehrersburg but does not independently bridge Christina Schreck to Christiana Clark.

19 results returned but none name a bride Christina/Christiana Schreck or Clark. No result contains 'Christina', 'Christiana', 'Schreck', or 'Berks' in its content. Results appear to be William Clark as witness or party to unrelated marriages where 'Christina' matched incidentally. No Pennsylvania marriage record found for William Clark + Christina/Christiana c. 1800-1820. If the couple married before moving to Ohio, the record may be in a county courthouse not yet digitized or indexed by FamilySearch.

20 results across 1820, 1830, and 1840 censuses. Key results: (1) 'William Clark, Senior' — 1820, Linton Township, Coshocton, Ohio (ark:/61903/1:1:XHGS-RMC) — the 'Senior' suffix confirms a son also named William in the same township; this is the likely Christiana's husband. (2) 'William Clark, Junior' — 1820, Linton Township (ark:/61903/1:1:XHGS-516). (3) 'William Clarke' — 1830, Linton, Coshocton (ark:/61903/1:1:XH5D-L95). Pre-1850 censuses show age brackets only, not names of other household members; a woman of Christiana's approximate age (37 in 1820, 47 in 1830) would appear in the household tally but cannot be identified by name. These census records confirm the William Clark family in Linton Township, Coshocton County, Ohio but cannot serve as the Schreck-to-Clark identity bridge directly.

query.note: identity-bridge search — Ohio marriage for William Clark + Christina/Christiana

log.notes: 18 results, all irrelevant — wrong era, wrong individuals. No Ohio marriage record found for William Clark + Christina/Christiana c. 1800-1820. Identity-bridge search exhausted for Ohio marriages.

log.notes: 6 results, all collection mismatches (1900 census, city directories from 1870s-1900s). No 1800 or 1810 census record found for William Clark in Berks County, Pennsylvania. FamilySearch did not return relevant early Pennsylvania census records for this query. Identity-bridge from PA census exhausted.

query.note: identity-bridge search — fulltext sweep for co-occurrence of Christiana Clark and Schreck

log.notes: 65 total results; top 5 examined. First result is a Pennsylvania cemetery transcription index (col. 3158832) listing unrelated Schrecks in Harris Township, Centre County — no connection to the Berks County Schreck family or Christiana Clark. Remaining results appear to be unrelated record sets that happen to contain both 'Clark' and 'Schreck' as surnames. No document found that co-names Christiana Clark and her Schreck maiden family. All autonomous FamilySearch identity-bridge paths now exhausted. Remaining approaches require physical repositories (Berks County Orphans Court / Adam Schreck estate) or external site captures (pli_003 FindAGrave, pli_005 Ancestry probate, pli_006 MyHeritage) — all awaiting user interaction.

sources.notes: Original Tulpehocken German Reformed church register image. Digital scan of original handwritten register. Transcribed by AI image reader (log_013). Confirms: birth 29 January 1783, baptism 2 March 1783, father Adam Schreck, mother Margaretha. The '3 February' date in src_002 was an indexer misread. This is the authoritative primary source.

assertions.informant: parents (Adam Schreck and Margreth), who presented the child for baptism and reported the birth date
assertions.rationale: Birth date 29 January 1783 recorded in church baptism register. Parents were present at birth and reported the date to the officiating minister — primary information from eyewitnesses. Consistent with the birth date already in the tree for GP3R-215.

assertions.place: Rehrersburg, Tulpehocken Township, Berks, Pennsylvania, United States
assertions.standard_place: Rehrersburg, Tulpehocken Township, Berks, Pennsylvania, United States
assertions.value: Baptized 2 March 1783, Rehrersburg, Tulpehocken Township, Berks, Pennsylvania
assertions.informant: officiating clergyman at Tulpehocken church who recorded the baptism contemporaneously
assertions.rationale: Baptism date and place recorded by officiating minister at time of the event. Primary information: the clergyman was present and acting in official duty. Matches tree entry for GP3R-215 (2 March 1783, Rehrersburg). Conflicting date in src_002 (3 Feb) flagged as conflict requiring image verification.

assertions.informant: Adam Schreck (father), present at baptism and identified as the father to the clergyman
assertions.rationale: The Tulpehocken church baptism register explicitly names Adam Schreck as father of Christina Schreck (born 29 Jan 1783, baptized 2 Mar 1783, Rehrersburg). As the father presenting his child for baptism, Adam Schreck had firsthand knowledge of his own identity. This is direct evidence answering q_002: the father of Christiana Clark (born Christina Schreck) was Adam Schreck. Corroborated by src_002 (also names Adam Schreck as father).

assertions.informant: Margreth (mother), present at baptism and identified as the mother to the clergyman
assertions.rationale: The baptism register names Margreth as the mother of Christina Schreck. Src_001 records only 'Margreth' (no surname); src_002 gives 'Margreth Schreck' (married surname). The mother was present at baptism — primary information. Direct evidence answering q_002: Christiana Clark's mother was named Margreth. Maiden surname not determinable from this source.

assertions.place: Rehrersburg, Tulpehocken Township, Berks, Pennsylvania, United States
assertions.standard_place: Rehrersburg, Tulpehocken Township, Berks, Pennsylvania, United States
assertions.value: Baptized 3 February 1783, Rehrersburg, Tulpehocken Township, Berks, Pennsylvania
assertions.informant: clergyman who recorded the original register entry, accessed via second index transcription
assertions.rationale: The '3 February 1783' baptism date from src_002 (6CTR-VMZX) was an indexer transcription error. Original church register image (log_013) confirms baptism date of 2 March 1783. Rejected in favor of a_002.

person_evidence.rationale: Birth date 29 January 1783 from Tulpehocken church register matches tree entry for GP3R-215 exactly. No other candidate among 240 Berks County baptism results shares this birth date with the matching baptism place.
Baptism 2 March 1783 at Rehrersburg confirmed by original register image (log_013). Matches tree entry on date and parish. 5-star FamilySearch automated match to GP3R-215.

Father Adam Schreck named in original Tulpehocken register for Christina Schreck baptized 2 March 1783. Christina Schreck = Christiana Clark (GP3R-215) by convergence of birth date, baptism date, and parish. Direct evidence of paternity.
Mother Margaretha named in original Tulpehocken register. Both indexed entries agree on the mother's given name. Direct evidence of maternity.
Adam Schreck (I1) named explicitly in original church register as father of Christina Schreck, baptized 2 March 1783, Rehrersburg.
Margreth/Margaretha (I2) named in original register as mother of Christina Schreck, baptized 2 March 1783, Rehrersburg.

conflicts.description: Two indexed entries of the same Tulpehocken church baptism give different dates: a_002 (src_001, 6CTR-RVHZ) gives 2 March 1783; a_005 (src_002, 6CTR-VMZX) gives 3 February 1783.
conflicts.independence_analysis: Both assertions derive from the same underlying Tulpehocken church register, accessed via two different transcription passes. They are dependent derivative assertions of a single original record. The conflict is an indexer error, not independent witnesses.
conflicts.weighing_analysis: The original church register image (log_013) outweighs both derivatives. The image records 'den 2ten Mertz' (2 March) as the baptism date and 'den 29ten Jenner' (29 January) as the birth date. The '3 February' in src_002 was a misread of the two-column format, likely conflating birth and baptism columns.
conflicts.resolution_rationale: Original register image is definitive. Baptism date is 2 March 1783. Assertion a_002 accepted; a_005 rejected as indexer error.
